conditions: YC2: lambda=0.55, omega_log=300.12K, Tc=3.81K (~3.8K). LaC2: lambda=0.54, omega_log=228.26K, Tc~2.4K. mu*=0.13 both.

conditions: delta-NbN: lambda=0.98 (sigma=0.18Ry), omega_log=269K, ThetaD=637K, Tc=18.2K (exp 17.3K). epsilon-NbN: lambda=0.16, Tc~0-1.08K (exp <1.77K). WC-NbN: lambda=0.11, Tc~0K. delta'-NbN: lambda=0.17, Tc~0K. mu*=0.10. ThetaD hexagonal phases 715-740K.

conditions: At 190 GPa: lambda=0.68, omega_log=565.1K. mu*=0.10 -> Tc=18.2K; mu*=0.13 -> Tc=13.7K. Across 180-200 GPa Tc rises monotonically, max near 195 GPa. Carbon-driven coupling (~91% of lambda from low-freq C modes).

Purely theoretical prediction (benzene not yet metallized/measured), no experimental Tc. Tc_computed=18.2K corresponds to mu*=0.10 at 190 GPa.

conditions: LaNiBN: lambda=0.52, omega_log=376K, sqrt<w^2>=184K, mu*=0.12, Tc~3.9K (exp 4.1K). LaPtBN: lambda=0.56, omega_log=294K, sqrt<w^2>=162K, mu*=0.10, Tc~5.4K (exp 6.7K). B1g mode softens 194->50 cm-1 Ni->Pt.

conditions: lambda=1.28, omega_log=215.9K, sqrt<w^2>=293.6K (acoustic moment, neutron), mu*=0.13, f1*f2 strong-coupling factors included, Tc=20.4K (exp 17.1K).

Tc=20.4K vs exp 17.1K. V sublattice dominates coupling (~200x Si).

Uses Allen-Dynes modified McMillan formula (Eqs.5-6) with omega_log/1.2 prefactor, no f1/f2 (lambda=0.68<1.5). Computed Tc=6.98K at mu*=0.10 in excellent agreement with experimental 7.0 K. Zone-center mode lambdas (Eg=1.22, B1g=1.94) are large but BZ-averaged lambda=0.68 (medium coupling). QE linear-response/Migdal-Eliashberg framework. mu* taken as 0.10, not computed.

conditions: With SOC (fully relativistic): lambda=0.93, omega_log=120.15K, Tc=7.05K; scalar-relativistic (no SOC): lambda=1.05, omega_log=107.17K, Tc=7.64K. mu*=0.11 in both.
notes: Allen-Dynes form of McMillan equation (Eq.5, omega_log/1.2 prefactor, no f1/f2). SOC reduces lambda by ~11% and hardens omega_log by ~12%. SOC-inclusive Tc=7.05K matches experimental Tc~=7.0K within 0.05K (Ref [67]). N(EF) 7.083->7.530 states/eV with SOC.

material: CS2H10 (CH4-intercalated H3S, host-guest hydride) under pressure
conditions: Cmc2_1 @150GPa: lambda=2.01, omega_log=799K, AD Tc~128K (mu*=0.13-0.10); P3m1 @50GPa: lambda=1.51, omega_log=651K, AD Tc~77-86K, scEliashberg Tc~85-95K; P3m1 @100GPa: lambda=1.21, omega_log=916K, AD Tc~79-90K, scE Tc~84-95K; P-3m1 @100GPa: lambda=0.63, omega_log=1113K, AD Tc~22-31K. mu*=0.10-0.13.
notes: Uses BOTH Allen-Dynes-modified McMillan AND self-consistent Eliashberg gap equations (T_c^scE). Large lambda values (Cmc2_1=2.01, P3m1=1.51) are in the strong-coupling f1/f2 regime; paper reports AD and scE values that diverge, justifying Eliashberg. H-derived states dominate N(EF). Experimental C-S-H phase judged unlikely to be these structures (P-V mismatch); no experimental Tc assigned to these computed phases. Could also be classified 'eliashberg' (dual method).

material: RM2B2C borocarbides: LaPt2B2C, YPd2B2C, YPt2B2C, LuNi2B2C (I4/mmm)
conditions: LaPt2B2C: lambda=0.78, omega_log=235K, Tc=10.40K; LuNi2B2C: lambda=0.83, omega_log=315K, Tc=15.94K; YPt2B2C: lambda=0.96, omega_log=175K, Tc=11.30K; YPd2B2C: lambda=1.48, omega_log=182K, Tc>~21K (high Tc material). mu*=0.10 all four.

YPd2B2C experimental Tc~=21K motivated study. ~67-70% of lambda from acoustic/low-freq optical modes. Single representative lambda/omega_log/Tc fields hold YPd2B2C (largest lambda); see conditions for all four.

conditions: Volume-dependent (Table I) across reduced volume 0.60<=v<=1.00: lambda grows up to ~2.8 under compression; computed Allen-Dynes Tc increases into the 10-20 K regime. Experimental Tc~17K at P=89GPa, ~19.5K at P=115GPa. mu*=0.15.
notes: Allen-Dynes modification of McMillan formula, mu*=0.15. Frequency moments omega_log, omega_1, omega_2 all computed from alpha^2F(omega) (specific numeric values not in node text; alpha^2F strongly weighted to 2-7 meV at high pressure). lambda reaches up to ~2.8 (very strong coupling) but paper still uses Allen-Dynes (no f1/f2 mentioned in nodes). Computed Tc 10-20K overlaps experimental 17-19.5K. Some imaginary harmonic modes at v=0.60 omitted from integral.

Allen-Dynes modification of McMillan formula (omega_log/1.2, no f1/f2), mu* in 0.12-0.15. Separate specific-heat-based estimate lambda_est~=0.99 (from C_el^exp/C_el^the=1+lambda; C_el^exp/T=18.0, C_el^the/T=9.01 mJ/mol K^2) - distinct from the EPC lambda=0.80 used in Tc. N(EF)=3.82 states/eV. No explicit experimental Tc value stated in nodes (only that experimental Tc exists and CdCNi3 has lower Tc than MgCNi3).

conditions: lambda_ep=0.99, omega_ln=65 cm^-1 (=93.5K), mu*=0.12 -> Tc~5.93K; mu* range 0.10-0.15 gives modest variation. Separate specific-heat estimate lambda_ep~0.63 from gamma^exp/gamma^calc=1+lambda.
notes: Paper explicitly uses the McMillan formula Tc=(hbar*omega_ln/1.2 kB)*exp[-1.04(1+lambda)/(lambda-mu*(1+0.62lambda))]; weak-point node states McMillan is used in place of full Allen-Dynes for lambda<1.5. Has the 1.04/0.62 McMillan-Allen-Dynes exponent but with omega_ln/1.2 prefactor and labeled 'McMillan formula'. omega_ln=65 cm^-1 converted to ~93.5K (65*1.4388). Computed Tc~5.9K vs experimental Tc~7K. lambda_ep=0.99 from ab initio alpha^2F; SOC negligible (<1% N(EF) change).

material: Monatomic metallic hydrogen, I4_1/amd (Cs-IV phase) under high pressure
conditions: P=480GPa: omega_log=1870.04K, lambda=2.17, Tc(mu*=0.10)=283.96K, Tc(mu*=0.13)=266.30K, N(EF)=0.48; P=802GPa: omega_log=2238.58K, lambda=1.68, N(EF)=0.42 (intermediate 539/608GPa also computed). Tc non-monotonic vs pressure. mu*=0.10 and 0.13.
notes: Allen-Dynes modified McMillan formula (omega_log/1.2, no f1/f2 in main result), mu*=0.10-0.13. Very strong coupling (lambda~1.68-2.17). Cross-checked with an alternative strong-coupling McMillan modification for large lambda (gives ~14% higher Tc at 802GPa/mu*=0.13), and notes non-adiabatic/vertex corrections may matter - but main Tc values are Allen-Dynes. No experimental Tc (theoretical hydrogen phase). Tc non-monotonic: omega_log rises but lambda and N(EF) fall with pressure.

material: Lanthanide hydrides CeH9 / PrH9 (P6_3/mmc) at 200 GPa (DFT+DMFT)
conditions: PrH9 @200GPa: DFT+DMFT+CSC Allen-Dynes Tc~70K vs plain DFT Tc~12K (mu*=0.1). CeH9 @200GPa: one-shot DFT+DMFT substantially increases AD Tc vs plain PBE-DFT (driven by increased omega_log and modified lambda); CSC tempers the shift. Numeric lambda/omega_log not given in node text.
notes: Allen-Dynes modified McMillan formula with mu*=0.1, but alpha^2F(omega) is DMFT-reweighted (Fermi-level delta functions replaced by interacting spectral weights A(k)). Method is DFT+DMFT(Hubbard-I)+CSC feeding DMFT+a2F into Allen-Dynes; phonons/EPC matrix elements kept at DFT/DFPT level. PrH9 lower Tc (~70K) than CeH9 due to larger 4f occupation suppressing f-weight at EF. Phonon q-grid 6x6x3. No experimental Tc in nodes.

material: nH-CaAlSi (1H/5H/6H stacking variants; also 2H/3H) under pressure
conditions: Computed (Allen-Dynes, mu*=0.10) for 2H stacking: P=0 omega_ln=160K lambda=0.60 Tc=3.66K; P=20kbar omega_ln=186K lambda=0.47 Tc=1.75K; P=40kbar lambda decreasing. Experimental single-crystal Tc: 1H=6.50K (dTc/dP~-0.03 K/kbar), 5H=5.95K (dTc/dP>0), 6H (dTc/dP>0). Full alpha^2F computed only for 1H; 5H/6H qualitative.
notes: Allen-Dynes (isotropic Migdal-Eliashberg) with mu*=0.10. Experimental susceptibility under pressure (Cu-Be cell, P<=10kbar) gives stacking-dependent dTc/dP sign. Numeric lambda/omega_ln/Tc only explicitly given for the hypothetical 2H stacking (placed in lambda/omega_log/Tc fields); 1H alpha^2F shows ultrasoft ~7meV peak splitting under pressure reducing lambda -> dTc/dP<0. mu* range 0.07-0.12. Computed (2H) and experimental (1H/5H/6H) are different stackings.
Not in LKM. 'gaia search lkm package --paper-id 812481926441992193' returns 'LKM API error 290011: paper graph: paper not found' on all attempts (raw-json and default formats).

material: HPC3 (2D hydrogenated phosphorus carbide monolayer, H1P2C6)
conditions: Unstrained: lambda=0.95, omega_log=482.84K, Tc=31.0K (basic Allen-Dynes, mu*=0.10). Biaxial tensile strain eps=1%: omega_log=497.73K lambda=1.05 Tc=37.3K; eps=2%: omega_log=475.57K lambda=1.24 Tc=44.4K; eps=3%: omega_log=402.84K lambda=1.65 with f1=1.102, f2=1.037 strong-coupling correction -> Tc=57.25K. Imaginary phonons for eps>~4%.
notes: Allen-Dynes modified McMillan, mu*=0.10. For eps=3% lambda=1.65>1.5 so the Allen-Dynes strong-coupling f1 (1.102) and shape f2 (1.037) correction factors are explicitly applied (Eqs.7-10), giving Tc=57.25K - hence allen-dynes-f1f2. Unstrained/1%/2% (lambda<1.5) use basic Allen-Dynes. Dominant coupling: C pz (pi) electrons to in-plane C/H modes. Theoretical 2D material, no experimental Tc.

Allen-Dynes modification of McMillan formula Tc=(omega_log/1.2)exp[-1.04(1+lambda)/(lambda-mu*(1+0.62lambda))], NO f1/f2 factors. lambda=0.17 from integrating ab initio alpha^2F(omega) (FP-LMTO linear response, Sternheimer). omega_log=459.25 K, mu*=0.14 (standard for transition metals). Tc<0.01 K, i.e. predicted non-superconducting. Independent specific-heat estimate gives lambda~0.2-0.25. Tc_computed reported as upper bound (<0.01 K).

conditions: Composition series Nb(1-x)B2: lambda_tot=0.334 for Nb0.96B2; lambda_tot=0.526 for Nb0.52B2; lambda evaluated at x=0,0.04,0.08,0.16,0.24,0.32,0.40,0.48 (nonmonotonic, decreases to x~0.25 then rises). notes: Allen-Dynes formula Tc=(omega_ln/1.2)exp[-1.04(1+lambda)/(lambda-mu*(1+0.62lambda))], no f1/f2. lambda computed via Gaspari-Gyorffy Hopfield parameter eta and lambda=sum eta_i/(M_i<omega_i^2>) from KKR-ASA-CPA. Note: two different omega_rms values appear in text: 464 cm^-1 in the eta/lambda conclusion and 400 cm^-1 (omega_rms) with omega_ln=494 K in the Tc conclusion. Computed Tc(x) curve qualitatively DISAGREES with experiment. omega2_K (504.6 K) = 464 cm^-1 x 1.4388; omega_log 494 K is the value entering Tc. mu*=0.09. No single computed Tc value stated; experimental behavior: onset by x=0.04, ~9.2 K near x~0.24, plateau to x~0.48.

conditions: Pnma Ca by pressure: 158 GPa lambda=0.896, omega_log=383 K; 161 GPa lambda=0.903, omega_log=408 K; 165 GPa lambda=0.915, omega_log=405 K; 172 GPa lambda=0.934, omega_log=396 K; 180 GPa lambda=0.887, omega_log=411 K. Lambda/omega_log values shown are for 172 GPa (the max-Tc condition). Computed Tc=24.7 K at 172 GPa agrees with experimental ~25 K at 161 GPa (modest pressure offset).

conditions: P-3m1 LaBH7 at 110 GPa: lambda=1.46, omega_log=837 K, Tc~93 K (Allen-Dynes modified McMillan, mu*=0.10). Fm-3m LaBH8 at 200 GPa: lambda=0.72, omega_log=1557 K, Tc~58 K (Allen-Dynes McMillan). LaBH8 near 50-55 GPa: lambda>=1.9 (up to ~2.3) - Eliashberg used when lambda>=1.5.
notes: Methodology: Allen-Dynes modified McMillan empirical formula used when lambda<=1.5; numerical isotropic Eliashberg equations solved when lambda>=1.5. mu*=0.10 fixed. omega_log=exp[(2/lambda)integral(dw/w)alpha2F ln w]. lambda=2 integral alpha2F/w dw. Reported lambda/omega_log/Tc are for LaBH7 at 110 GPa (the headline value). Predicted high-Tc, no experimental Tc reported (prediction paper). Recorded method=allen-dynes-basic for the headline LaBH7/LaBH8 values; paper also uses eliashberg for high-lambda LaBH8 (lambda>=1.9) cases.

conditions: SrPt3P lambda=1.06, omega_log=7.01 meV (=81.35 K), N(EF)=3.95 states/eV. CaPt3P lambda=0.82, LaPt3P lambda=0.78 (Tc lower for Ca and La). mu* range 0.10-0.13 (two Tc values quoted for mu*=0.10 and 0.13). Tc evaluated via McMillan-Allen-Dynes formula Tc=(omega_log/1.20)exp[-1.04(1+lambda)/(lambda-mu*-0.62*lambda*mu*)].
notes: McMillan-Allen-Dynes formula (no f1/f2 factors), Tc=(omega_log/1.20)exp[-1.04(1+lambda)/(lambda-mu*-0.62*lambda*mu*)]. lambda=1.06, omega_log=7.01 meV=81.35 K from numerical integration of Eliashberg alpha^2F(omega). Strong-coupling s-wave phonon-mediated. Two Tc values for mu*=0.10 and 0.13 but the explicit numeric Tc value was truncated in the node text and not captured here. omega_log_K = 7.01 meV x 11.604.

conditions: Computed (DFT linear response + Allen-Dynes): Sc-III at 100 GPa lambda=0.79, omega_log=261 K; Sc-IV at 170 GPa and Sc-V at 260 GPa also computed (lambda/omega_log values truncated in node text). mu* range 0.10-0.13. Experimental Tc(P): 8.2 K at 64 GPa (Sc-II), 18.8 K at 98 GPa, dips in Sc-III, rises in Sc-IV, accelerates near Sc-IV->Sc-V ~210 GPa, max measured Tc=37.6 K at ~243 GPa (range 64-261 GPa).
notes: Allen-Dynes modified McMillan formula Tc=(omega_log/1.20)exp[-1.04(1+lambda)/(lambda-mu*(1+0.62lambda))], no f1/f2. lambda/omega_log from DFT linear-response phonon+EPC (alpha^2F). mu*=0.10-0.13 bracket. Lambda/omega_log recorded are Sc-III at 100 GPa (only fully-stated values; Sc-IV/Sc-V numbers truncated). Experimental Tc up to 37.6 K (transport, DAC). Pressure-enhanced Tc attributed to phonon softening raising lambda. Tc_computed not fully captured (node text truncated).

conditions: Ambient pressure: Tc=0.73 K (Allen-Dynes, mu*=0.1). Hydrostatic compression to 30 GPa: Tc decreases monotonically 0.73 -> 0.32 K (N(EF) down, alpha2F blueshift, omega_log up, lambda down). Uniaxial c-axis: Tc increases to max 6.34 K at 17 GPa (lambda up, omega_log down, acoustic softening); above this harmonic phonons go imaginary (instability). Raman modes Eg~105, A1g~150 cm^-1 (vs exp 102, 145). Experiment: no superconductivity above 1.1 K (no measured Tc given).
notes: Allen-Dynes-modified McMillan formula Tc=(omega_log/1.2)exp[-1.04(1+lambda)/(lambda-mu*-0.62*lambda*mu*)], mu*=0.1, no f1/f2. lambda=2 integral alpha2F/w dw from computed Eliashberg function (DFPT, LDA-PZ). Numeric lambda/omega_log values not stated per-condition in node text (only Tc values). LDA structure a=3.677 A, c=6.331 A, B0=51.1 GPa. Tc_experimental: only an upper bound stated (no SC above 1.1 K) so left null.

conditions: lambda_ph=0.54, omega_log(omega_ph)=143 K. McMillan/Dynes formula: Tc=2.4 K for mu*=0.10, Tc=1.6 K for mu*=0.13 (range). mu* range 0.10-0.13. Paramagnon-extended formula reproduces exp Tc~2.2 K only if lambda_sf~0.03. Experimental Tc~2.2 K.
notes: McMillan/Dynes formula Tc=(omega_ph/1.20)exp[-1.04(1+lambda_eff)/(lambda_eff-mu*_eff(1+0.62*lambda_eff))], no f1/f2. lambda_ph=0.54 computed via McMillan-Hopfield eta_i and lambda_i=eta_i/(M_i<omega_i^2>) (rigid muffin-tin, KKR-LDA), summed over sites (Mo ~35%, Sb ~65%). omega_log=omega_ph=143 K from full phonon spectrum. Computed Tc=2.4 K (mu*=0.10) reproduces experimental Tc~2.2 K. Tc_computed recorded as 2.4 K (mu*=0.10 case); 1.6 K for mu*=0.13.

material: Phosphorus (sc, modulated P-IV/CM, sh phases; 100-150 GPa)
conditions: sc phase (>100 GPa): lambda~0.50, Tc~4 K. CM (commensurate modulated P-IV, gamma=0.25) at ~120 GPa: lambda~0.40, Tc~2 K. sh phase: lambda~0.52. omega_log decreases with pressure within sc but increases across sc->CM->sh. mu*=0.1 fixed. CM phase Tc~2 K (consistent with experiment no abrupt rise above 4 K). lambda recorded for CM phase (0.40); sc lambda~0.50, sh lambda~0.52. omega_log values not given numerically in node text. Tc_computed recorded as ~2 K (CM phase headline); sc ~4 K.

conditions: Ordered P-6m2 (Ce,La)H9 at 200 GPa: lambda=3.0, omega_log=327 K, mu*=0.1, f1=1.22, f2=1.20, Tc=87 K.
notes: Allen-Dynes-modified McMillan WITH multiplicative strong-coupling prefactors: Tc = f1*f2*(omega_log/1.2)exp[-1.04(1+lambda)/(lambda-mu*(1+0.62lambda))]. f1=1.22, f2=1.20 explicitly applied (because lambda=3.0 is very large). lambda=3.0 from integrating isotropic Eliashberg alpha^2F(omega) (DFPT, harmonic - note imaginary K-point acoustic modes present). omega_log=327 K, mu*=0.1. Tc=87 K. Prediction paper, no experimental Tc. AIMD at 203 K stabilizes structure.

conditions: Trigonal BaSi2 ambient: lambda~0.33, omega_log~240 K, mu*=0.1, computed Tc~0.3 K vs experimental 6.8 K. To match experiment would require lambda~0.6.
notes: Allen-Dynes modification of McMillan formula Tc=(omega_log/1.2)exp[-1.04(1+lambda)/(lambda-mu*(1+0.62lambda))], mu*=0.1, no f1/f2. lambda=0.33, omega_log=240 K from DFPT alpha^2F. Computed Tc~0.3 K is an order of magnitude BELOW experimental Tc=6.8 K (severe quantitative failure attributed to isotropic approximation). Inverting formula gives required lambda~0.6 (~2x computed).

material: Alkali metals under high pressure (Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs)
conditions: Series Li/Na/K/Rb/Cs up to ~90 GPa. lambda increases markedly with pressure in Li,K,Rb,Cs; stays small in Na (lambda~0.25, negligible Tc, predicted non-superconducting). mu*=0.13 and 0.20 (two bracketing values used). lambda=eta/(M<omega^2>)=N(eF)<I^2>/(M<omega^2>) via Gaspari-Gyorffy RMTA; <omega^2>=C*B(V)*V^(1/3) calibrated by omega^2=(1/2)Theta_D^2.
notes: Uses McMillan strong-coupling expression Tc=(<omega>/1.2)exp[-1.04(1+lambda)/(lambda-mu*(1+0.62lambda))] with a single AVERAGED phonon frequency <omega> (NOT a logarithmic-average omega_log from alpha^2F; this is the McMillan-style averaged-frequency / Debye-scaled approach, <omega^2>=(1/2)Theta_D^2). lambda from Hopfield parameter eta=N(eF)<I^2> (RMTA/Gaspari-Gyorffy, APW-LDA), NOT from integrated Eliashberg. mu*=0.13 and 0.20. lambda=0.25 recorded for Na. No single numeric Tc stated per element in node text (Na predicted negligible). Method tagged mcmillan due to averaged-frequency/Debye phonon scaling and RMTA Hopfield route rather than DFPT alpha^2F omega_log.

McMillan formula T_c = (Theta_D/1.45) exp(...) with Theta_D=265 K, lambda=0.806, mu*=0.10. lambda from lambda = N(E_F)<I^2>/(M<omega^2>) using Debye-model <omega^2>=0.5*Theta_D^2 (so sqrt(<omega^2>) = sqrt(0.5)*265 = 187.4 K). N(E_F)<I^2>=0.3897 eV/A^2 via Evans simple-metal reduction of Gaspari-Gyorffy. APW band structure at compressed V/V0=0.52. Computed Tc=11.03 K exceeds experimental ~7 K; agreement called 'fairly satisfactory'.

conditions: x=0.1: N(EF)=0.16 states/eV/(Pb/Bi)/spin, mu*=0.082. x=0: N(EF)=0.14, mu*=0.103. Mode-resolved lambda_i = alpha_i N(EF) V_d,i^2/(M_i omega_i^2) for 3 optical modes (O-breathing ~15 meV V_d=1.0; Pb/Bi ferroelectric ~50 K V_d=1.0; rigid-rotation ~1.5 meV V_d=0.25). Total lambda=sum lambda_i.
notes: WARNING: LKM en_title metadata is MISMATCHED -- it reads 'An example of the inverse problem for bound-continuum electronic transitions in diatomic molecules' but the entire graph content is unambiguously the Ba(Pb1-xBix)O3 optical-phonon superconductivity paper. Used the graph content. Paper labels it 'McMillan/Allen-Dynes expression' Tc=(<omega>/1.20)exp[-1.04(1+lambda)/(lambda-mu*(1+0.62lambda))] -- prefactor <omega>/1.20, no f1/f2, so allen-dynes-basic by prefactor. Key wrinkle: the characteristic frequency <omega> is operationally replaced by the second-moment <omega^2>^(1/2)=(sum lambda_i omega_i^2/sum lambda_i)^(1/2) -- i.e. omega2 IS the frequency moment used here (specific numeric value not given in nodes). Also gives a weak-coupling limit Tc=1.14<omega>exp[-1/(lambda-mu*)]. mu* = Morel-Anderson values 0.082 (x=0.1) / 0.103 (x=0), NOT defaults. lambda from deformation-potential model (not alpha^2F integral). No single numeric Tc shown in the returned nodes (text cut off at 'Using the computed total couplings and phonon mome...').

conditions: bcc Li: lambda=0.51 (+/-0.08), <omega>=180 K, Tc=1.73 K; 9R Li: lambda=0.41, <omega>=200 K, Tc=0.58 K. With spin-fluctuation lambda_sp=0.1: Tc drops to ~0.19 K (bcc) and ~8.8e-3 K (9R). mu*=0.12 (Thomas-Fermi estimate). Experiment: no superconductivity in Li down to 6 mK.
notes: Uses the McMillan expression Tc=<omega>/1.2 * exp[-1.04(1+lambda)/(lambda-mu*(1+0.62 lambda))] with the COUPLING-WEIGHTED average phonon frequency <omega> (NOT omega_log) as prefactor - classified as McMillan form (frequency-weighted average, no Debye Theta_D prefactor, no Allen-Dynes f1/f2). The <omega> here is a coupling-weighted average (sum lambda_qv omega_qv / sum lambda_qv) = 180 K (bcc), 200 K (9R), reported in K. mu*=0.12 from Thomas-Fermi. Spin-fluctuation paramagnon term lambda_sp=0.1 added in a modified McMillan-like expression to suppress Tc, consistent with the experimental ABSENCE of superconductivity (down to 6 mK). Frozen-phonon self-consistent pseudopotential. No measured Tc (Li is non-superconducting in this regime).
